pr.title: Decompile 9 fighter, library, game, and menu functions
pr.author: johnwinston

summary: Decompiled a mixed batch of fighter, game-mode, library, player-stat, and menu functions, replacing several `/// #symbol` stubs and `UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS` prototypes with typed C implementations. The PR body says all 9 functions matched; the provided diff evidence covers 8 decompiled functions plus related prototype/struct fixes, while the listed trophy viewer function `un_80307828` is mentioned in the body but not present in the changed-file list or diff excerpt.
what_changed: Implemented `fn_800DA1D8(Fighter_GObj* gobj)` in `ftCo_Attack100.c`, setting up CatchWait after a grab: clears ground velocity, changes motion state to `ftCo_MS_CatchWait`, installs accessory and damage callbacks, stores a capturedamage joint, spawns effect `0x41D`, updates fighter state helpers, and notifies the victim via `fn_800DB6C8`.
Updated `ftCo_Attack100.h` prototype for `fn_800DA1D8` from unknown return/params to `void fn_800DA1D8(Fighter_GObj* gobj)`.

Implemented `ftCh_Damage2_Anim(HSD_GObj* gobj)` in `ftCh_Init.c`, including `mp/mplib.h`; on animation end it compares Crazy Hand position against `mpFloorGetRight`, either transitions through `ftCh_Init_8015737C` if off the right side or computes recovery position fields, changes to `ftMh_MS_Damage`, restarts animation, and plays SFX `0x4E207`.

Implemented `ftPp_SpecialAirHiThrow_0_Anim(Fighter_GObj* gobj)` in `ftPp_SpecialS.c`, handling Popo aerial up-special throw completion, using Ice Climber attributes for `ftCo_80096900`, checking command variable `cmd_vars[1]`, querying Nana with `Player_GetEntityAtIndex` and `ftNn_Init_8012309C`, optionally triggering `ftPp_SpecialHi_8012280C`, and incrementing `mv.pp.unk_80123954.x0` before `ftPp_SpecialS_80120FE0`.

Implemented `ftCo_800C0134(Fighter* fp)` in `ftcolanim.c`, resetting collision animation state with `lb_80014498`, calling a character-specific callback from `ftData_UnkMotionStates4`, checking `ftCo_800C53E4`, and selecting the correct color animation storage among `x488`, `x508`, and `x408` via `lb_800144C8` and `Fighter_804D653C` metadata.
Changed `fn_8016F160` in `gm_16AE.c/.h` from `void` to `int` and made it return `pl_80039418(arg0, arg1)`, enabling callers such as the newly decompiled event loop to test its result.
Typed `fn_8016FFD4` in `gm_16F1.h` as `void fn_8016FFD4(struct lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t*, int, int)` instead of `UNK_RET fn_8016FFD4(UNK_PARAMS)`.

Implemented `fn_8017F1B8(void)` in `gmregclear.c`, looping over 256 registered event slots, checking `fn_8016F1F0`, input masks from `fn_8017F008` and `fn_8016F180`, dispatching through either `fn_8016F160` or `fn_801701C0`, calling `fn_8016F140` on success, then forwarding the final mask to `fn_8016FFD4(gm_8016B774(), mask, 0)`.

Implemented `fn_8001F06C(THPDecComp* data)` in `lbmthp.c`, disabling interrupts, advancing THP counters, handling wrap/end behavior based on fields such as `unk_40`, `unk_68`, `unk_78`, `unk_88`, `unk_104`, `unk_108`, and `unk_10C`, restoring interrupts, and returning `1`.

Refined `THPDecComp` in `lbmthp.h` by splitting padding into actual signed fields `unk_6C`, `unk_108`, and `unk_10C`, changing `unk_68` to `s32`, and typing `fn_8001F06C` as `s32 fn_8001F06C(THPDecComp* data)`.

Implemented `mnName_80238A04(HSD_GObj* gobj, u8 target, u8 flag)` in `mnname.c`, requesting and playing animations for name-entry JObjs `0x18..0x1A`, then choosing highlight/confirm animation frames from `mnName_803ED538` for the `prev_gx` JObj based on `target` and `flag`.
Updated `mnname.h` prototype for `mnName_80238A04` from unknown params to `void mnName_80238A04(HSD_GObj* gobj, u8 target, u8 flag)`.

Implemented `pl_80037DF4(HSD_GObj* gobj, union Struct2070* ev)` in `pltrick.c`, copying the event locally and incrementing player action-stat counters for total attacks, attack type index `x2073`, thrown items, aerials, specials, and two additional counters `x1A0_count` and `x1A4_count`.

PR body also lists `un_80307828` in `toy.c` as a decompiled trophy viewer reset function, but that file/function is not included in the provided changed-file list or diff excerpt, so details are only weakly evidenced by the body text.
smart_moves: Converted unknown prototypes alongside implementations, which allowed new decompiled code to make typed calls and expose real return values, notably `fn_8016F160` returning an `int` for the game event dispatcher.
Updated struct layout in `THPDecComp` only where fields were needed by the decompiled interrupt-safe THP counter routine, preserving offsets while replacing padding with named signed fields.
Used local pragma blocks around matching-sensitive functions, including `#pragma push`/`#pragma pop` after review feedback, so optimizer/inline settings did not leak into unrelated functions.
For `fn_8001F06C`, preserved interrupt safety with `OSDisableInterrupts` and `OSRestoreInterrupts`, matching the expected critical-section behavior for THP playback state updates.

For fighter functions, reused existing engine helper calls and state fields rather than open-coding behavior: `Fighter_ChangeMotionState`, `ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining`, `efAsync_Spawn`, `Player_GetEntityAtIndex`, `HSD_JObjReqAnimAll`, and `HSD_JObjAnimAll`.

Matched nontrivial control flow using explicit temporaries and casts where needed, such as the `found` variable in Popo's Nana check, `(u8)` mask casts in `fn_8017F1B8`, and a `goto end` in the THP handler.
decomp_lessons: When decompiling a dispatcher, propagate accurate return types into wrapper functions first; `fn_8016F160` had to return the result of `pl_80039418` so `fn_8017F1B8` could match its conditional dispatch logic.

Struct padding should be split into real fields only with offset evidence from matched code; `THPDecComp` demonstrates replacing broad padding with signed counters at `0x6C`, `0x108`, and `0x10C` while keeping surrounding offsets stable.
Pragma settings used for matching must be scoped with `#pragma push` and `#pragma pop`; an unscoped `#pragma dont_inline on` can incorrectly affect every later function in a file.
Matching fighter animation routines often depends on preserving state-machine helper call order, callback assignments, and motion-state parameters exactly.

Menu animation routines can match by identifying JObj traversal and animation-frame tables rather than naming every structure field; here `mnName_803ED538` supplied frame choices based on `target` and `flag`.
For low-level library code touching shared decoder state, interrupt disable/restore structure is part of the behavioral and matching evidence, not just a safety wrapper.
Use existing unknown field names when semantics are not yet proven. This PR kept names such as `unk_108`, `mv.pp.unk_80123954.x0`, and `count_x1A0`, avoiding overclaiming.
naming_conventions: Function names retained project-address style where semantics were still unknown or only partially known, such as `fn_800DA1D8`, `fn_8017F1B8`, and `fn_8001F06C`.

Known subsystem prefixes were preserved: `ftCo_` for common fighter, `ftCh_` for Crazy Hand, `ftPp_` for Popo, `gm`/`fn_8016` for game-mode code, `mnName_` for name-entry menu, `pl_` for player/stat tracking, and `lbmthp` for THP library code.
Headers were updated from `UNK_RET name(UNK_PARAMS)` to concrete signatures once implementations established argument and return types.
Struct fields remained conservative where semantics were unclear, using `unk_` field names and offset-like names rather than speculative names.
Typedefs and project standard types were used consistently in signatures and locals: `Fighter_GObj*`, `HSD_GObj*`, `HSD_JObj*`, `Fighter*`, `Vec3`, `s32`, `u8`, `BOOL`, and `THPDecComp*`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `PAD_STACK(8)` in `fn_800DA1D8` and `PAD_STACK(40)` in `ftPp_SpecialAirHiThrow_0_Anim`, indicating stack-shape padding was needed for matching.
Scoped `#pragma auto_inline off` and `#pragma global_optimizer off` around `ftCo_800C0134` to control compiler behavior for a sensitive match.
Scoped `#pragma dont_inline on` around `mnName_80238A04`; initial review caught that it needed push/pop wrapping to avoid affecting the rest of `mnname.c`.
Used explicit `goto end` in `fn_8001F06C` to preserve the interrupt-restore exit path and likely match original branch structure.
Used explicit casts such as `(u8) fn_8016F180(i)`, `(u32) fn_8016F160(0, i)`, and `(u8) target`/`(u8) flag` to reproduce comparison and masking behavior.
Introduced temporary locals such as `arg1`, `entry`, `mask`, `local_ev`, `jobj`, `jobj2`, and `found` where the diff suggests they were needed for matching codegen.
Used assignment inside an argument expression in `ftCh_Damage2_Anim` for `fp->mv.ch.unk0.xC.z = ftCh_Init_804DA070`, a code-shape tactic likely required by the matching output.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: The added `#pragma dont_inline on` lacked a preceding `#pragma push` and trailing `#pragma pop`, so it incorrectly affected all following functions in the file.

review_feedback.author: johnwinston
review_feedback.feedback: Fixed by adding `#pragma push` / `#pragma pop` wrapping around the matching pragma.

searchable_terms: decompile 9 fighter library game menu functions
follow_up_queries: Check the full PR diff or repository history for the missing `toy.c` / `un_80307828` changes mentioned in the PR body but absent from the provided changed-file list.
Look for later PRs that rename `fn_800DA1D8`, `fn_8017F1B8`, `fn_8001F06C`, or newly exposed `THPDecComp` fields after more semantics were established.
Search for other unscoped matching pragmas in menu files after this review pattern: `#pragma dont_inline on` without nearby `#pragma push` and `#pragma pop`.
Investigate `THPDecComp` field names at offsets `0x68`, `0x6C`, `0x108`, and `0x10C` once more THP decoder functions are decompiled.
Use `fn_8017F1B8` as a reference when decompiling nearby `gmregclear` event-registration functions that share `fn_8016F1F0`, `fn_8016F180`, `fn_801701C0`, and `fn_8016FFD4`.
